behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watching behavior (recursive watch coverage), not just path logic. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of path filtering can pass while the daemon is misconfigured to watch only the root. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: this depends on real OS file events for nested directories. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML call or agent pipeline; it is strictly filesystem watching.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp input/output directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output is produced for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text focuses on dedup time-window logic for the same path; this can fail entirely within one in-memory component with controlled clock/time inputs. MOCK-MISS: no required cross-module boundary is stated. Rejected higher levels: no explicit OS/network/subprocess dependency in this behavior text, and no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedup component with a test clock and window, submit the same normalized file path twice within the window, assert first is accepted and second is rejected; advance clock beyond window and assert acceptance again.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS-emitted duplicate filesystem events. REAL-INFRA: exact duplicate-event patterns are OS/watcher-runtime behavior. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic single-callback tests can miss real duplicate bursts/coalescing differences. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependence and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-file write+rename), capture processing invocations, and assert one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS-level watcher lifecycle behavior (delete/recreate inode/path changes) during a live process. REAL-INFRA: requires real directory removal/recreation events and re-subscription behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher callbacks can miss broken reattachment logic that fails only with real FS semantics. Rejected higher levels: behavior has no AI/ML/model decision content.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, remove the watched directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on filesystem timestamp metadata under near-simultaneous arrival, which is tied to real FS timestamp granularity and event timing. MOCK-MISS: pure sorting tests can pass while real metadata precision/ties break ordering in production. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML call or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously in a real watched directory with controlled creation times, run daemon, record processing order, and assert it matches ascending creation timestamp rule (including deterministic tie handling if defined).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real concurrent write/read timing and filesystem event timing. REAL-INFRA: requires actual partial writes and completion detection against OS semantics. MOCK-HIDE: mocked 'file complete' signals can hide races where daemon reads incomplete content. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependence or agentic sequencing in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Open a file in the watched directory, write partial content and keep handle open, verify daemon does not process yet, finish write/close handle, then assert processing occurs once with full final content.
